Idiom (Dan): B脿谩 k蓻虁 蓳a虁n y脿.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause; subject + completive aspect + object metaphor
Figurative Meaning: To act recklessly without considering consequences
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family correction, community disputes
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to hunters firing arrows blindly in forest tradition
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): 蓳脿藧 k蓻虁 蓳a虁n j脿
First Example Sentence: 脥 b脿谩 k蓻虁 蓳a虁n y脿, g蓴虁蓴虁n d蓴虁 l蓴虂.
Second Example Sentence: 脌 b脿谩 k蓻虁 蓳a虁n y脿 y茅, m蓻虂 z蓴虁蓴虁 l蓴虂.
Third Example Sentence: B脿谩 k蓻虁 蓳a虁n y脿 n脿, 蓳蓻虁 k蓴虂.

Idiom (Dan): K蓴虂蓴虁 z脿 蓳蓻虁.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal metaphor; noun phrase with emphatic particle
Figurative Meaning: A person who pretends to be strong but is fragile
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from clay pots that look solid but crack easily
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): k蓴虂藧 z脿 蓳蓻虁
First Example Sentence: 脌 k蓴虂蓴虁 z脿 蓳蓻虁 y茅, d蓴虁 k蓻虂 m蓻虂.
Second Example Sentence: 脥 k蓴虂蓴虁 z脿 蓳蓻虁, g蓴虁n l蓴虁.
Third Example Sentence: K蓴虂蓴虁 z脿 蓳蓻虁 n脿, m蓻虂 y茅.

Idiom (Dan): M蓻虂 l蓴虁 z蓴虁蓴虁 k蓴虂.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with intensifier; habitual aspect
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to traditional silence during village mediation
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): m蓻虂 l蓴虁 z蓴虁藧 k蓴虂
First Example Sentence: 脌 m蓻虂 l蓴虁 z蓴虁蓴虁 k蓴虂 y茅, g蓴虁n k蓻虁.
Second Example Sentence: 脥 m蓻虂 l蓴虁 z蓴虁蓴虁 k蓴虂 n脿, m蓴虁 l蓴虂.
Third Example Sentence: M蓻虂 l蓴虁 z蓴虁蓴虁 k蓴虂 y茅, 蓳蓻虁 d蓴虁.

Idiom (Dan): Z脿 k蓻虁 g蓴虁n 蓳a虁.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Evokes stepping into thorn bushes during farming
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): z脿 k蓻虁 g蓴虁n 蓳a虁
First Example Sentence: 脥 z脿 k蓻虁 g蓴虁n 蓳a虁 y茅.
Second Example Sentence: 脌 z脿 k蓻虁 g蓴虁n 蓳a虁 n脿, m蓻虂 l蓴虁.
Third Example Sentence: Z脿 k蓻虁 g蓴虁n 蓳a虁, 蓳蓻虁 k蓴虂.

Idiom (Dan): Y茅 蓳蓻虁 l蓴虁 k蓴虂.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Copular construction; subject + copula + metaphorical complement
Figurative Meaning: Things are not as they seem
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in folktales involving trickster figures
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): j茅 蓳蓻虁 l蓴虁 k蓴虂
First Example Sentence: Y茅 蓳蓻虁 l蓴虁 k蓴虂 y茅, m蓻虂 d蓴虁.
Second Example Sentence: 脌 k蓻虂 y茅 蓳蓻虁 l蓴虁 k蓴虂.
Third Example Sentence: 脥 m蓻虂, y茅 蓳蓻虁 l蓴虁 k蓴虂.

Idiom (Dan): D蓴虁 蓳a虁n k蓴虂蓴虁.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in initiation narratives in Dan communities
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): d蓴虁 蓳a虁n k蓴虂藧
First Example Sentence: D蓴虁 蓳a虁n k蓴虂蓴虁 y茅, g蓴虁n m蓻虂.
Second Example Sentence: 脌 l蓴虁 d蓴虁 蓳a虁n k蓴虂蓴虁.
Third Example Sentence: 脥 m蓻虂 d蓴虁 蓳a虁n k蓴虂蓴虁 y茅.

Idiom (Dan): K蓻虁 y脿 z蓴虁蓴虁.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): k蓻虁 j脿 z蓴虁藧
First Example Sentence: 脥 k蓻虁 y脿 z蓴虁蓴虁 y茅.
Second Example Sentence: 脌 k蓻虁 y脿 z蓴虁蓴虁 n脿, m蓻虂 l蓴虁.
Third Example Sentence: K蓻虁 y脿 z蓴虁蓴虁, 蓳蓻虁 d蓴虁.

Idiom (Dan): M蓴虁 k蓴虂 l蓴虁 蓳蓻虁.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): m蓴虁 k蓴虂 l蓴虁 蓳蓻虁
First Example Sentence: 脌 m蓴虁 k蓴虂 l蓴虁 蓳蓻虁 y茅.
Second Example Sentence: 脥 m蓴虁 k蓴虂 l蓴虁 蓳蓻虁 n脿.
Third Example Sentence: M蓴虁 k蓴虂 l蓴虁 蓳蓻虁, g蓴虁n l蓴虁.

Idiom (Dan): G蓴虁n y茅 k蓴虂蓴虁.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: The truth will eventually come out
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional arbitration sayings in Dan villages
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): g蓴虁n j茅 k蓴虂藧
First Example Sentence: G蓴虁n y茅 k蓴虂蓴虁 y茅, m蓻虂 l蓴虁.
Second Example Sentence: 脥 d蓴虁, g蓴虁n y茅 k蓴虂蓴虁.
Third Example Sentence: 脌 k蓻虂 g蓴虁n y茅 k蓴虂蓴虁.

Idiom (Dan): 蓳茞虁 z脿 l蓴虁 m蓻虂.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to secret alliances in historical clan conflicts
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Dan): 蓳蓻虁 z脿 l蓴虁 m蓻虂
First Example Sentence: 脌 蓳蓻虁 z脿 l蓴虁 m蓻虂 y茅.
Second Example Sentence: 脥 蓳蓻虁 z脿 l蓴虁 m蓻虂 n脿, g蓴虁n l蓴虁.
Third Example Sentence: 蓳蓻虁 z脿 l蓴虁 m蓻虂, m蓻虂 d蓴虁.
